item.title: World-First Work From Home Laws Threaten Melbourne’s Economy
item.description: Melbourne workers will soon get the legal right to work from home two days a week, a move opposed by small and big businesses.

item.title: Colombia’s Next Rate Meeting at Risk After Minister Walkout
item.description: Colombia’s finance chief’s decision to abandon the latest central bank meeting in protest of an interest rate hike approved by the board is raising questions about policymakers’ legal ability to conduct monetary policy, putting their next gathering in less than a month at risk.

item.title: Venezuelan Oil Exports Hit 6-Year High as India Fills China Gap
item.description: Indian refiners are emerging as the largest buyers of Venezuelan crude oil, filling the void left by former top importer China as it cuts purchases following the US’ move to control the Latin American nation’s oil sales.

item.title: Cofco Books First Argentina Corn Cargo to China in Over 15 Years
item.description: Cofco International Ltd. said it is loading a bulk cargo of Argentine corn to China, in what will be the first such shipment in more than 15 years as the two countries expand agricultural trade.

item.title: Plastic Bottle Makers Get Pinched by War-Driven Forces Majeures
item.description: US manufacturers of soda bottles, peanut butter jars, sandwich bags and just about anything else made of plastic are getting squeezed as the Iran war chokes off supplies of key components.

item.title: Canada Banks Have $633 Million in Exposure to Struggling Lender
item.description: Canadian banks have about C$879 million ($633 million) in loan exposure to struggling subprime-lender Goeasy Ltd., according to calculations based on the firm’s latest financial filings.

item.title: Stellantis in Talks to Make Chinese EVs at Idled Canadian Plant
item.description: Stellantis NV is discussing options for building electric vehicles in Canada with its Chinese partner, Zhejiang Leapmotor Technology Co., according to people familiar with the matter, a sign of how quickly the auto industry is being reshaped after Canada opened the door to companies from the world’s largest car market.

item.title: Stocks Enter Danger Zone: Late-Week Trading Has Crushed S&P 500
item.description: Five weeks into a Middle East war that’s sending shockwaves through the global economy, the US stock market has settled into a predictable pattern. It starts the week on a strong note, drifts sideways toward the middle of the week and then, like clockwork every Thursday and Friday, collapses.

item.title: Sonos Makes Marketing Cuts as New CMO Reshapes Division
item.description: Sonos Inc. eliminated jobs on its marketing team Wednesday, with new Chief Marketing Officer Colleen DeCourcy telling employees that the division “had grown into something too diffuse to move at the speed this brand needs.”

item.title: Steeper West Texas Gas Discounts Are Outlier of Energy Markets
item.description: Since the US-Israel attacks against Iran broke out just over a month ago, most global energy markets have rallied on concerns over supply disruptions. But in West Texas, natural gas prices are dirt cheap — and now look set to stay lower for longer.

item.title: IMF, World Bank and IEA to Coordinate Efforts Around Iran War
item.description: The International Monetary Fund, the World Bank Group and the International Energy Agency said they’ll work together to respond to the economic fallout of the Iran war.

item.title: Banks Launch Debt Sale to Refinance Chemicals Firm Archroma
item.description: Banks have launched a more than $1 billion debt sale for Swiss chemicals firm Archroma to refinance its loan coming due next year, after the firm showed improving earnings amid a difficult period in the sector.

item.title: Oil Fund Cayler Posts 18% Gain in March as Iran War Roils Market
item.description: Oil fund Cayler Capital surged more than 18% in March in its second best month on record as the Iran war upended global energy markets and created avenues to profit.

item.title: Novo Digs in to Protect Its No. 1 Spot in Obesity Pill Market
item.description: Earlier this year, Novo Nordisk A/S pulled off the best obesity-drug launch ever with its Wegovy pill. Now it’s focused on building on more than 600,000 prescriptions as Eli Lilly & Co. threatens to siphon patients away with a new pill of its own.

item.title: A 12,000-Mile Journey Shows the World’s Scramble for Diesel
item.description: Soaring fuel prices from the Iran war are forcing oil traders into longer, stranger journeys, sending cargoes on routes that would normally make little economic sense.

item.title: Gold Traders Made a Record $3.9 Billion in 2025, Coalition Says
item.description: Precious metals trading desks around the world had their best year ever in 2025 as dramatic dislocations and surging trading volumes provided more opportunities than ever to make money.

item.title: MIT Seeks to Unleash Faculty and Student Startups Amid AI Boom
item.description: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology is exploring ways to make it easier for faculty and students to pursue their own startups and cash in on soaring demand for artificial intelligence.

item.title: Accor Agrees to Sell Hotel Stake to Blackstone Consortium
item.description: Accor SA has signed a memorandum of understanding to sell its stake in Essendi to a consortium including Blackstone Inc. for up to €975 million ($1.1 billion).

item.title: Bank of Canada Agreed Iran War Shouldn’t Overtake Other Risks
item.description: While the Bank of Canada’s governing council spent considerable time discussing the inflation risks posed by the war in Iran, officials agreed that they “should not lose sight” of other major economic headwinds when they held interest rates steady last month.

item.title: Hasbro Pulls Some Systems Offline as Breach Inquiry Continues
item.description: The toy company Hasbro Inc. said it discovered unauthorized access in its network on Saturday and has taken certain systems offline.

item.title: Oracle Data Center Nears $16 Billion Financing After Twisty Path
item.description: Related Digital is finalizing $16 billion of financing for a giant Oracle Corp. data center, after months of stop-and-start negotiations with investors.

item.title: Mamdani Spars With NYC City Council Over $127 Billion Budget
item.description: New York City Council Speaker Julie Menin outlined a budget proposal that she said would close a $6 billion deficit without raising taxes, cutting services or raiding the city’s savings — a plan Mayor Zohran Mamdani immediately deemed “unrealistic.”

item.title: Super Micro Co-Founder Pleads Not Guilty in China Smuggling Case
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw pleaded not guilty Wednesday in New York to charges that he helped illegally divert billions of dollars in Nvidia Corp.-powered servers to China in a hearing in Manhattan.

item.title: Bain’s Gross Says CEOs Get AI Wrong, Focus on Tech Over Strategy
item.description: Artificial intelligence is being misapplied by executives who treat it as a technology rollout rather than a fundamental rethink of how businesses operate, Bain Capital Managing Partner David Gross said in a Bloomberg TV interview.

item.title: After Decade of Slumps, Argentine Peso Becomes Surprise ‘Haven’
item.description: For more than a decade, investors could rely on the Argentine peso to do one thing — plunge. Now, as the war in Iran roils global markets, it’s thriving.

item.title: Nigerian Banks Raise $3.4 Billion to Meet New Capital Standards
item.description: Nigerian lenders raised 4.7 trillion naira ($3.4 billion) in fresh capital to meet a deadline to strengthen their balance sheets, the Central Bank of Nigeria said.

item.title: Brazil’s B3 Eyes Election Bets as It Enters Prediction Markets
item.description: Brazil’s stock exchange B3 SA is moving into prediction markets with a set of contracts tied to key local assets and economic indicators. It is also considering offering election-linked products, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Anthropic Rushes to Limit the Leak of Claude Code Source Code
item.description: Anthropic PBC is rushing to address the inadvertent release of internal source code behind Claude Code, an AI-powered assistant that has become a key moneymaker for the company.

item.title: Boeing Shares Soar On US Deal To Triple Patriot Seeker Output
item.description: Boeing Co. shares rose as much as 5.6% after the Pentagon announced a framework deal with the company to triple production of a crucial Patriot missile component over seven years.

item.title: Half of South Africa Wastewater Plants Deemed in Critical State
item.description: Almost half of South Africa’s wastewater facilities are in a critical state, raising the risk of them discharging untreated water into rivers and the environment.
